item.title: Return of the Meme: 1123 (In those Amcient Days)
item.description:
Good morning, O imgur. Let us take this bread.
I have been obsessed with every empire mentioned in this meme at some point.
To be fair, this is pretty much just an average conversation with the clerks at the 7-11 affiliate in Sedona.
This legit is a couple of my exes.
In those ancient days, in those remote nights, in those days of yore, when the necessary things had been brought into existence, when bread had been tasted for the first time in the shrines of the Land... For your enjoyment: https://youtu.be/QUcTsFe1PVs?si=eQkEovNFjmqi8BZm
Someone basically made their ancient grandma into a DnD character and I'm all for that.
Loons do have a delightfully spooky sound.
Marsh Wren doin the splits.
This is a hopefully handy chart for my pain peeps, like @kaeldra
Cambrian seas.
Dilophosaurus and Scuttelosaurus, Mark Hallett.
Me, every damn morning.
A guy practicing in a copy of the Dendra Panopoly.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Oh, Pippin. Glory Pippin. I love you too.)

item.title: Bruce Willis
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: "Batman: The Animated Series" premiered 32 years ago today in 1992
item.description:
From the original "pilot", made in the summer of 1991. THE DARK KNIGHT'S FIRST NIGHT. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hMucplM3CUk It was used to pitch the series (utilizing Danny Elfman's score since they hadn't yet gotten Shirley Walker to compose any original music for the series) to the execs at Fox Kids. Animated quickly in Canada by friends, the show itself would eventually be made by master animation studios in Japan and South Korea.
The very first episode was "On Leather Wings". Featuring a showdown between the Caped Crusader and the MAN-BAT. A fight made vividly possible by the Japanese animation studio "Spectrum".
The Batman himself, along with the cast that played his rogues gallery. (That should be "Paul Williams", an error by the magazine this photo came from)
Adam West and Kevin Conroy, taken in '92 during recording for the episode "Beware the Grey Ghost"
Like Mark Hamill (known at that time only for playing Luke Skywalker in STAR WARS) as the clown prince of crime... THE JOKER.
The series rose to critical acclaim after the episode "Heart of Ice" (which aired on Sept 7th). It won the show an Emmy Award for Best Writing in an Animated Series. From a laughing stock villain (originally called Mister Zero in the comics), MR. FREEZE was reimagined by the series as a tragic foe. Seeking vengeance not only for his condition, but the loss of his beloved wife... whom he sought to save with cryogenic freezing. This new origin eventually carried over into the comics, the first of many changes TAS would make to the Batman mythos.
The famed singer and songwriter Paul Williams would probably be the last person you would consider to voice a villain, and yet through casting by Andrea Romano... he was the perfect voice to play THE PENGUIN.
Richard Moll, best known as Bull on the sitcom "Night Court", was cast as the voice of Harvey Dent. The lifelong friend of Bruce Wayne plagued by mental health problems... that culiminated in his tragic descent into villainy as TWO-FACE.
Ron Perlman played the voice of the disfigured actor turned shapeshifting monster CLAYFACE. The energy and fluidity of his transformations made his appearances some of the series' very best episodes, thanks to the animation work of the Japanese studio TMS Entertainment (who among other projects also made the anime film AKIRA).
One the series' saddest endings, from the episode "Baby Doll"
Originally just a one-off henchwoman, Harley Quinn debuted in the episode "Joker's Favor" [on Sept 11th, 1992]. And she was created solely because the writers realized they needed another bad guy to wheel in the cake that the Joker was hiding in. Dini had based Harley and his friend Arleen, but when it came to casting the character... he managed to convince voice-acting director Andrea Romano to cast Arleen. The rest as they say is history.
The execs were impressed by the character, and this combined with her popularity with the viewers allowed Harley to become a recurring villain on the series. Instead of just being a walk-on role. Appearing in several more episodes like "Almost Got 'Im", "The Man Who Killed Batman" and "The Laughing Fish".
Roddy McDowall was one of my favorite voice-actors from the series, playing the villainous MAD HATTER (a tragic character who became warped by his technological skills, brainwashing and manipulating others for his own gain).
Character actor John Glover voiced the ego-manical RIDDLER (for only a few episodes, because the writers had trouble coming up with clever riddles). He would later go on to star in the live-action WB series "Smallville", playing the Lionel the father of Lex Luthor.
Robert Costanzo voiced the belligerent DETECTIVE HARVEY BULLOCK. He gets his hands dirty, but is mostly honesy... he just doesn't like or trust costumed vigilantes like "the Commish".
Bob Hasting, an actor known mostly for comedic roles on decades of sitcom appearances, did some of his best (as well as dramatic) work playing the voice of COMMISSIONER GORDON in TAS. He also did voice work in the late 70s and 80s, one as SUPERBOY and several others while guest-starring on SCOOBY-DOO. One such episode was alongside Mark Hamill, just before his film debut in "STAR WARS"... the two would reunite when he was cast as the Joker twenty years later. The less said about the fourth season's drastic art style change... the better.
Officer (later Detective) Renee Montoya was also an original character created for the show, who like Harley would eventually be introduced in the comics. As one of the few allies (besides Commissioner Gordon) Batman had in the GCPD. She'd also eventually become one of the first of many openly gay DC characters.
The late great David Warner played the definitive voice of "he who is known as RA'S AL GHUL". The immortal criminal mastermind... Batman's Moriarty, so to speak.
When Harley met Ivy, a classic supervillain team-up that would evolve from comedic friendship... into a romantic relationship. Still a villainous one, but a major improvement compared to the toxic Joker.
Adrienne Barbeau was the sultry voice of Selina Kyle, aka CATWOMAN in TAS.
One of my all-time favorite moments from TAS, especially the way Conroy says that finishing line.
Eventually she was introduced into the comics themselves, debuting in Batman Adventures # 12 - https://i.imgur.com/3HxiyjN.jpg Becoming a staple of the Batman mythos for the next three decades to come.
George Dzundza played the dual role of the ARNOLD WESKER the meek VENTRILOQUIST, as well as his ruthless alter ego SCARFACE (a personality so split he needs the use of a dummy to interact with people). Dzundza would later go on to star in SUPERMAN: TAS, playing Daily Planet editor-in-chief Perry White.
TAS not only changed the face of animation, bringing writing and action not seen in a decade of cartoons prior. It also created an entire universe of shows and films that would span the next 15 years. The DC Animated Universe
BATGIRL was played first by Melissa Gilbert (Little House on the Prarie), then by Mary Kay Bergman in the direct-to-video film SUBZERO. Tara Strong would play the voice for the longest span in the rest of the DCAU.
This a special shout-out dedicated to @ponypink , who loved the character so much that for nearly 2 years straight they posted "Harley-A-Day" tribute posts. I'm only sorry they couldn't stick around for Harley's B-DAY.
Also a special HAPPY BIRTHDAY to Michael Keaton! Sharing a B-Day with Batman: TAS' premiere seems almost poetic. Without him and the groundwork laid Tim Burton, the series wouldn't have been possible.

Yarr
Yay gay dads!
You have caused confusion and delay, and killed fuck tonnes of people, Homelander.
I should get some Jager.
USE FOSS, PIRATE SHIT, CONVERT YOUR GAMES AND DVDS INTO DIGITAL FILES AND STORE THEM ON A LARGE EXTERNAL HARD DRIVE! FUCK THE POLICE!
Pretzel!
Yes
oh no poor Ted!
Jeff!
Oh look, another Sonic the Hedgehog situation
HELL YEAH!

item.title: Trying to change things up
item.description: Got a Planet Fitness membership, started working with a trainer. Horrific childhood abuse made food my coping mechanism of choice. I've done intense therapy the past 5 years, healed a lot. I'm 38 fucking years old and I'm no longer in a shitty marriage with a narcissist. I've spent my life taking care of everyone else. It's time to take care of me now. Any advice, validation, encouragement? I'm currently feeling a weird mix of excitement, pride, and shame that it's gotten this bad. Thanks, Imgur. I love you all! <3

item.title: Winona Ryder remembers Jeff Bridges being a gentleman during an early-’90s audition - Refusing to Kiss Her in ‘Fearless’ Audition Due to Their Age Difference
item.description: Winona Ryder remembers Jeff Bridges being a gentleman during an early-’90s audition. The actress, now 52, was asked about auditions for roles she didn’t ultimately get during Josh Horowitz‘s “Happy Sad Confused” podcast and brought up her encounter with Bridges, now 76. “I remember because I was also auditioning for Fearless with Jeff Bridges, and I remember that audition. It was really around the same time [I auditioned for The Hudsucker Proxy], I think,” she recalled on the Monday, September 2, episode of the podcast. “Jeff Bridges, who I love, wouldn’t kiss me because I was, like, too young.” Part of long interview on YT: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dvG-qcP7Kf8
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin
